title: Google AI Overview Citations From Top-10 Pages Dropped From 76% to 38%
summary: Ahrefs study of 863,000 keywords found only 38% of pages cited in Google AI Overviews also rank in top 10, down from 76% seven months prior. BrightEdge puts overlap at 17%. YouTube is now single most-cited domain at 18.2% of non-top-100 citations. Gemini 3 upgrade on January 27, 2026 likely contributed. Organic CTR with AI Overviews fell up to 61%.

title: Google Making Links in AI Mode and AI Overviews More Direct
summary: Google announced 5 updates to AI Overviews and AI Mode links on May 6, 2026. Links now placed right next to relevant generated text. Desktop hover shows website name or page title. Subscription links highlighted with early testing showing users significantly more likely to click. Community perspectives from forums and social media now surfaced with creator names and handles.

title: The Execution Gap Holding Back AI in Modern Marketing Teams
summary: Forbes reports that AI budgets are growing and pilots multiplying but results feel underwhelming. Wrike research: 82% of knowledge workers use AI, 38% use 3-5 AI tools weekly, but 96% say it would be valuable if AI tools could share context and work together. The issue is not tools but operating models designed for sequential handoffs and functional silos. Adding AI to broken processes makes chaos happen faster.

title: MiCA Website & Crypto Advertising Rules 2026: CASP Compliance Guide
summary: In 2026, CASP websites and marketing communications entered the core regulatory perimeter of the EU. European supervisory authorities check every page of public websites, ad banners, and social media posts. Crypto advertising under MiCA is now a strictly controlled sphere of financial promotion. Google only allows crypto ads from MiCA-authorized CASPs since April 2025. Marketing to EU residents can trigger CASP licensing obligation. CASPs responsible for third-party partner marketing. Fines up to 5M EUR or 5% annual turnover.

title: The Agency Model Collapsed — Here's the Integrated Growth Architecture That Replaced It
summary: Tapp Network argues the agency model collapsed 2013-2023 through three waves: social democratized distribution, remote work decentralized skills, AI commoditized production. 68% of brands moved services in-house (Marketing Scoop), 82% built internal agencies (ANA), only 11% believe current agency model meets future needs. 83% of marketing leaders will cut spending when AI automates content creation fully (eMarketer). Cross-functional collaboration yields 20% higher revenue growth.

title: Is AI Quietly Widening Your GTM Execution Gap?
summary: Heinz Marketing reports AI improves productivity but simultaneously introduces new complexity across workflows, alignment, and pipeline execution. More activity, more assets, more automation — and yet more confusion, rework, and friction. Gartner reports vendor-offered AI agents often fail to meet promised business performance. Over 40% of agentic AI projects predicted to be canceled by end of 2027 due to cost, unclear value, or risk. Only 31% of prioritized AI use cases reach full production. 72% of AI investments fail to deliver.

title: 2026 Will Expose Broken Marketing Workflows Being Masked by AI
summary: SmartBrief warns the wow phase of AI is waning and the how phase has begun. Two years buying tools and marveling at speed but adding AI to a broken process makes chaos faster. Wrike research: 82% knowledge workers using AI, 96% want AI tools to share context. 90% of employees see value in AI agents coordinating across tools. The problem is work fragmentation and orchestration, not AI capability.
